name: GeGLU
oneliner: GLU variant using GELU activation instead of Swish — performs nearly identically to SwiGLU, used in T5 v1.1 and Flan-T5.
explanation: From the same Shazeer (2020) paper. GELU(x) = x·$\Phi$(x) where $\Phi$ is Gaussian CDF. Practically indistinguishable from SwiGLU in quality (both ~21.3 perplexity vs ReLU's ~22.5 in Shazeer's T5 experiments). The ecosystem split: LLaMA-inspired models standardized on SwiGLU, T5/Flan-T5 family uses GeGLU. GELU was already standard in BERT/GPT-2, so GeGLU was a natural extension. Less common in decoder-only LLMs post-2023.
fundamentals: GeGLU(x) = (GELU(xW_gate) ⊙ xW_up)W_down. GELU(z) $\approx$ z·0.5·(1 + tanh($\sqrt{2/π}$·(z + 0.044715$z^3$))). Same parameter count as SwiGLU: 3 $\times$ d_model $\times$ d_ff. Swish vs GELU: $\Phi$(x) has slightly steeper transition than $\sigma$(x), negligible practical difference.
